volumeTitle: Casina. The Casket Comedy. Curculio. Epidicus. The Two Menaechmuses
label: The Casket Comedy (Section 1.1.100 - Section 2.1.505)

sections.label: Section 1.1.100
sections.original: sua cognata Lemniensis , quae habitat hic in proxumo .
sections.translation: a Lemnian lady , his relation , who is living here hard by ( pointing to DEMIPHO ’ S house ) ; for his father has compelled him . Now my mother is enraged with me , because I didn ’ t return home to her , when I came to know of this matter , that he was about to take another as his wife .

sections.label: Section 1.1.103b
sections.original: Nihil amori iniuriumst .
sections.translation: Nothing ’ s unfair in love .

sections.label: Section 1.1.104
sections.original: Nunc te amabo ut hanc hic unum triduom hoc solum sinas
sections.translation: Now , I entreat you that you ’ ll let her ( pointing to GYMNASIUM ) be here only for the next three days ,

sections.label: Section 1.1.105
sections.original: esse et hic servare apud me . nam ad matrem accersita sum .
sections.translation: and keep house for me ; for I ’ ve been sent for to my mother ’ s house .

sections.label: Section 1.1.106
sections.original: Quamquam istud mihi erit molestum triduom , et damnum dabis ,
sections.translation: Although this will be a troublesome three days for me , and you ’ ll be causing me a loss , I ’ ll do so .

sections.label: Section 1.1.107b
sections.original: Facis benigne et amice . sed tu , Gymnasium mea ,
sections.translation: You act kindly and like a friend . But you , my dear Gymnasium , if in my absence Alcesimarchus shall come , don ’ t you chide him roughly ; however he has deserved of myself , still he has my affections ;

sections.label: Section 1.1.110
sections.original: sed , amabo , tranquille : ne quid , quod illi doleat , dixeris .
sections.translation: but , prithee , act gently , so that you mayn ’ t say anything that may cause him pain . Take the keys ( giving them to her ) ; if you have need to take out anything for use , take it out . I wish to go —

sections.label: Section 1.1.112b
sections.original: Vt mi excivisti lacrimas .
sections.translation: ( weeping .) How you have drawn tears from me .

sections.label: Section 1.1.112c
sections.original: Gymnasium mea ,
sections.translation: My dear Gymnasium , kindly , farewell .

sections.label: Section 1.1.113b
sections.original: Cura te , amabo . sicine immunda , obsecro ,
sections.translation: Take care of yourself , there ’ s a dear . Prithee , will you go in this dishabille ?

sections.label: Section 1.1.114b
sections.original: Immundas fortunas aequom est squalorem sequi .
sections.translation: It ’ s right that such neglect should attend upon my prospects thus disarranged .

sections.label: Section 1.1.115
sections.original: Amiculum hoc sustolle saltem .
sections.translation: At least do lift up that outer garment [*] .

sections.label: Section 1.1.115b
sections.original: Sine trahi , cum egomet trahor .
sections.translation: Let it be dragged , while I myself am being dragged down .

sections.label: Section 1.1.116
sections.original: Quando ita tibi lubet , vale atque salve .
sections.translation: Since so it pleases you , fare you well and prosper .

sections.label: Section 1.1.116b
sections.original: Si possim , velim .—
sections.translation: If I could , I would .

sections.label: Section 1.1.117
sections.original: Numquid me vis , mater , intro quin eam ? ecastor mihi
sections.translation: Mother , do you wish anything of me , before I go indoors ? Upon my faith , to me she does seem to be in love .

sections.label: Section 1.1.118b
sections.original: Istoc ergo auris graviter obtundo tuas ,
sections.translation: For this reason , then , it is , that I ’ m repeatedly dinning it into your ears , not to be in love with any man . Go in-doors .

sections.label: Section 1.1.119b
sections.original: Numquid me vis ?
sections.translation: Do you wish anything of me ?

sections.label: Section 1.1.119c
sections.original: Vt valeas .
sections.translation: That you may fare well .

sections.label: Section 1.1.119d
sections.original: Vale .—
sections.translation: Fare you well .

sections.label: Section 1.2.120
sections.original: Idem míhi magnae quod parti est vitium mulierum
sections.translation: ( to the AUDIENCE .) It ’ s the same fault with myself as with a great part of us women who are following this calling ; who , as soon as ever we have got our load of food , are forthwith full of talk ; more than is enough do we say .

sections.label: Section 1.2.123
sections.original: nam ego illanc olim , quae hinc flens abiit , parvolam
sections.translation: But once upon a time , that girl , who has gone hence in tears , from a lane I carried off a little child exposed .

sections.label: Section 1.2.125
sections.original: adulescens quidam hic est adprime nobilis
sections.translation: There is here a certain youth , of the highest rank ;

sections.label: Section 1.2.126
sections.original: quin ego nunc quia sum onusta mea ex sententia
sections.translation: Why , myself now , inasmuch as I ’ m filled to my heart ’ s content , and because I ’ ve charged myself quite full of the choicest of wine , it pleases me to use my tongue more at freedom ; to my misfortune I can ’ t keep silent on that which it were necessary to be silent upon .

sections.label: Section 1.2.130
sections.original: Sicyone , summo genere ; ei vivit pater .
sections.translation: his father , of a very high family , is living at Sicyon [*] ;

sections.label: Section 1.2.131
sections.original: is amore misere hanc deperit mulierculam ,
sections.translation: he is dying desperately in love for this young woman , who has just . now gone hence in tears ; on the other hand , she is smitten with love .

sections.label: Section 1.2.133
sections.original: eam meae ego amicae dono huic meretrici dedi ,
sections.translation: I made a present of her to my friend , this Courtesan : who had often made mention of it to me

sections.label: Section 1.2.135
sections.original: puerum aut puellam alicunde ut reperirem sibi ,
sections.translation: that somewhere I must find for her a boy or a girl , just born , that she herself might pass it off as her own . As soon as ever the opportunity befell me , I immediately granted her request in that which she had asked of me .

sections.label: Section 1.2.139
sections.original: postquam eam puellam á me accepit , ilico
sections.translation: After she had received this female child from me , at once

sections.label: Section 1.2.140
sections.original: eandem puellam peperit quam a me acceperat ,
sections.translation: she was brought to bed of the same female child which she had received from me , without the aid of a midwife and without pain , just as other women bring forth , who seek a trouble to themselves ; but she said that her lover was a foreigner , and that by reason of that circumstance she was palming it off .

sections.label: Section 1.2.145
sections.original: id duae nos solae scimus : ego quae illi dedi
sections.translation: This , we two alone are aware of , I who gave the child to her , and she who received it from me ; ( to the AUDIENCE ) except yourselves , indeed . Thus was this affair managed ; if any occasion should arise , I wish you to remember this circumstance . I ’ m off home .

sections.label: Section 1.3.149
sections.original: Vtrumque haec , et multiloqua et multibiba , est anus .
sections.translation: ( To the AUDIENCE .) This old woman is both a much-talker and a much-tippler .

sections.label: Section 1.3.150
sections.original: satin víx reliquit deo quod loqueretur loci ,
sections.translation: Isn ’ t it the fact that she has hardly left room to a Divinity for him to speak , so much has she forestalled him in talking about the substitution of this girl ? But if she had held her tongue , still I was about to mention it — a God , who could do it better ; for my name is Help . Now ( to the AUDIENCE ) lend your attention ,

sections.label: Section 1.3.155
sections.original: ut ego argumentum hoc vobis plane perputem .
sections.translation: that I may clearly explain this plot to you . Some time since , at Sicyon , there was the Festival of Bacchus ; a merchant of Lemnos [*] came hither to the games , and he , an ungovernable young man , ravished a maiden [*] in the dark , in the street , at the dead of night .

sections.label: Section 1.3.160
sections.original: is ubi malam rem scit se meruisse , ilico
sections.translation: He , as he knew that he was deserving of a heavy punishment , at once found shelter with his heels , and made off for Lemnos , where he then lived . She whom he had ravished , the ninth ensuing month completed , brought forth a daughter here [*] . Since she did not know the person guilty of this deed , who he was ,

sections.label: Section 1.3.165
sections.original: paternum servom sui participat consili ,
sections.translation: she made the servant [*] of her father partaker of her counsels , and gave to that servant the child to be exposed to death . He exposed it ; this woman took up the child ; that servant , who had exposed it , secretly took note whither or to what house she carried away the child .

sections.label: Section 1.3.170
sections.original: ut eampse vos audistis confiterier ,
sections.translation: As you have heard her own self confess , she gave this child to the Courtesan Melaenis ; and she brought her up as being her own daughter , honestly and virtuously . But then , this Lemnian married a neighbour there , his relation , for his wife .

sections.label: Section 1.3.175
sections.original: ea diem suom obiit , facta morigera est viro .
sections.translation: She departed this life ; there she was compliant to her husband . After he had performed the due obsequies to his wife , at once he removed hither ; here he married for his wife that same woman [*] whom formerly , when a maid , he ravished . When he understood that it was she whom he had ravished ,

sections.label: Section 1.3.180
sections.original: illa illi dicit , eius se ex iniuria
sections.translation: she told him that , in consequence of the violation , she had brought forth a daughter , and had at once given her to a servant to be exposed . He forthwith ordered this same servant to make enquiries , if anyhow he could discover who had taken it up . Now to that task

sections.label: Section 1.3.185
sections.original: operam usque assiduo servos dat , si possiet
sections.translation: is the servant always assiduously devoting his attention , if he can find out that Courtesan , whom formerly , when he himself exposed her , he from his hidingplace had seen take her up . Now , what remains unpaid , I wish to discharge , that my name may be struck out , so that I mayn ’ t remain a debtor .

sections.label: Section 1.3.190
sections.original: adulescens hic est Sicyoni , ei vivit pater ;
sections.translation: A young man [*] is here at Sicyon , his father is alive ; with affection he distractedly dotes upon this exposed girl , who just now went hence in tears unto her mother ; and she loves him in return , which is the most delightful love of all . As human matters go , nothing is granted for everlasting :

sections.label: Section 1.3.195
sections.original: pater adulescenti dare volt uxorem ; hoc ubi
sections.translation: the father is wishful to give the young man a wife . When the mother [*] came to know of this , she ordered her to be sent for home . Thus have these matters come to pass . Kindly fare you well , and conquer by inborn valour , as you have done before ; defend your allies , both ancient ones and new ;

sections.label: Section 1.3.200
sections.original: augete auxilia vostra iustis legibus ,
sections.translation: increase resources by your righteous laws ; destroy your foes ; laud and laurels gather ; that , conquered by you , the Pœni [*] may suffer the penalty .

sections.label: Section 2.1.203
sections.original: Credo égo Amorem primum ápud homines carníficinam comméntum .
sections.translation: I do believe that Love was the first to invent torture among mankind . This conjecture do I form from myself at home , not to go seek it out of doors ;

sections.label: Section 2.1.205
sections.original: qui omnés homines supero atque ántideo cruciábilitatibus ánimi .
sections.translation: I , who surpass all men , exceed them in the pangs of my feelings . I ’ m tossed , tormented , agitated , goaded , whirled on the wheel of love in my misery , I ’ m deprived of sensation , carried one way , carried another way , I ’ m torn and rent asunder ; such clouded faculties of mind have I ,

sections.label: Section 2.1.211
sections.original: ubi sum , íbi non sum , úbi non sum , íbist animus ,
sections.translation: where I am , there I am not ; where I am not , there my thoughts are ; to such a degree have I now all kinds of feelings in me ; what I like , then all at once I like not the same ; so much does love trifle with me changing my mind ,

sections.label: Section 2.1.216
sections.original: fugat , ágit , appetit , raptát , retinet ,
sections.translation: drive me , pursue , desire , and seize for itself , retain , trepan , and promise ; what it gives , it gives not ; it deludes me ; what this moment it has persuaded me , it now dissuades me from ; what it has dissuaded me from , it now points out to me that same .

sections.label: Section 2.1.221
sections.original: maritúmis moribus mecum experitur
sections.translation: After the manner of the sea is it experienced by me ; so much does it distract my enamoured feelings ; and only in that , in my misery , I do not sink utterly , is there any evil removed from me thus ruined ;

sections.label: Section 2.1.225
sections.original: ita páter apud villam détinuit
sections.translation: in such a way has my father detained me these six days running in the country , at his house there ; nor has it been allowed me in the meantime to visit my mistress . Isn ’ t this dreadful to relate ?

sections.label: Section 2.1.492
sections.original: Eo facetu'sfacetus es quia tibi aliast sponsa locuples Lemnia .
sections.translation: Are you joking for this reason , because you ’ ve got another wife engaged , a rich lady of Lemnos ? Have her then ! We are neither of a family so great as you are , nor is our wealth so substantial as yours ; but still

sections.label: Section 2.1.495
sections.original: hau metuo ne ius iurandum nostrum quisquam culpitet :
sections.translation: I have no fears that any one will impeach our oath ; you then , if you shall feel any pain , will know for what reason you do feel pain .

sections.label: Section 2.1.497
sections.original: Di me perdant —
sections.translation: May the Gods confound me —

sections.label: Section 2.1.497b
sections.original: Quodcumque optes , tibi velim contingere .
sections.translation: Whatever you wish for , I desire it may befall you .

sections.label: Section 2.1.498
sections.original: Si illam uxorem duxero umquam , mihi quam despondit pater .
sections.translation: If ever I ’ ll marry that wife which my father has engaged for me .

sections.label: Section 2.1.499
sections.original: Et me , si umquam tibi uxorem filiam dedero meam .
sections.translation: And me , if ever I give you my daughter for a wife .

sections.label: Section 2.1.500
sections.original: Patierin me periurare ?
sections.translation: Will you allow me to be forsworn ?

sections.label: Section 2.1.500b
sections.original: Pol te aliquanto facilius ,
sections.translation: Yes , and a little more easily than myself and my affairs to go to ruin , and my daughter to be trifled with . Begone ! go seek where there is confidence enough in your oaths ; here now , with us , Alcesimarchus , you ’ ve renounced your title [*] to our friendship .

sections.label: Section 2.1.504
sections.original: Face semel periclum .
sections.translation: Make trial of me but once .

sections.label: Section 2.1.504b
sections.original: Feci saepe , quod factum queror .
sections.translation: I have made that trial full oft ; which I lament has been so made .

sections.label: Section 2.1.505
sections.original: Redde mi illam .
sections.translation: Give her back to me .
